prompt: Create a warm vintage photo-collage on a 16:9 horizontal canvas with an off-white textured paper background. The scene is a golden-hour outdoor field at sunset, with soft orange haze, distant dark hills, blurred dry grass, strong lens flares, and a nostalgic 1970s film look. Place one large central torn-edge photograph slightly overlapping the background, showing a curly-haired adult man standing waist-up in the field, wearing a cream suede jacket over a patterned brown paisley button-up shirt; cover the central portrait face with a flat opaque medium brown square, as if anonymized. Add exactly 4 small instant Polaroid prints clustered on the right side, overlapping at different angles: one top-right tilted clockwise, one mid-right tilted slightly counterclockwise, one lower-center tilted counterclockwise, and one bottom-right tilted clockwise. Each Polaroid contains a black-and-white close portrait of the same curly-haired subject in similar clothing, and each face is obscured by a soft gray square blur. Use cream Polaroid borders with slight yellow aging, subtle paper shadows, worn edges, dust specks, and realistic layered depth. Keep the composition cinematic, analog, tactile, and scrapbook-like, with no readable text, no logos, and no watermark.
